entries.definition:
l–f–f (of the thighs) to be fleshy; to gather together, to wrap
up; large crowd of a mixture of people, thicket of trees; to stutter.
Of this root, three forms occur once each in the Qur’an: الْتَفَّ
ʾiltaffa; أَلْفَاف ʾalfāf and لَفِيف lafīf.

الْتَفَّ ʾiltaffa [v. VIII, intrans.] to wind around, to join closely
together *(75:29) وَالْتَفَّتِ السَّاقُ بِالسَّاقِ and when death overtakes the
person [lit. and leg is intertwined with leg].

أَلْفَاف ʾalfāf [pl. of لَفّ luff which is, in turn, pl. of quasi-act. part.
لَفَّاء laffāʾ] intertwined, conflated, thickly interlacing, tangled
(78:15–16) لِنُخْرِجَ بِهِ حَبًّا وَنَبَاتًا وَجَنَّاتٍ أَلْفَافًا to bring forth with it grain,
plants and gardens of interlacing foliage.

لَفِيف lafīf [quasi-act./pass. part.] diversified crowd, a mixed
group (17:104) وَجِئْنَا بِكُمْ لَفِيفًا and when the promise of the Hereafter is
fulfilled, We shall bring you [out] as a mixed group.
